title: Superficial Dissection of Shoulder
description: A comprehensive anterior and posterior view of a dissected shoulder. It demonstrates the structural relationships between the shoulder girdle bones, the rotator cuff muscles, and the intricate network of the brachial plexus cords transitioning into terminal nerves within the axilla.
clinical_notes: The complex neurovascular bundle passing through the axilla is vulnerable during severe traction injuries or crutch compression. Understanding the spatial relationship of the cords to the axillary artery helps OTs map motor and sensory deficits back to specific brachial plexus lesions.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 11 – The Muscular System & Chapter 13 – The Peripheral Nervous System
